text:
Admission Date:  2015-08-22              Discharge Date:   2018-05-08

Date of Birth:  2003-06-09             Sex:   F

Service: NEUROSURGERY

Allergies:
Patient recorded as having No Known Allergies to Drugs

Attending:James
Chief Complaint:
transfer OSH s/p fall


Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
none

History of Present Illness:
HPI: 51 y.o. F s/p fall at home after consumption of EtOH.
+LOC.
Patient does not recall event.  Per witnesses, she tripped over
fireplace and hit head on cement of fireplace.  No seizure
activity.  Minimal nausea.  No other history of falls/syncopal
events.  At OSH patient loaded with Dilantin.  No F/C/V/CP/SOB.



Past Medical History:
PMHx: Elevated chol, HTN, DM, Panic attacks, Depression


Social History:
Social Hx: +EtOH 1-2drinks/day, 20 pack year smoking hx


Family History:
Family Hx:non contrib


Physical Exam:
PHYSICAL EXAM:
O: T:97.1  BP: 124/85   HR: 83    R 20     O2Sats 99
Gen: WD/WN, comfortable, NAD.
HEENT: Large ecchymosis R eye, Pupils: 4-2mm b/l EOMs intact b/l
Neck: Supple.
Lungs: CTA bilaterally.
Cardiac: RRR. S1/S2.
Abd: Soft, NT, BS+
Extrem: Warm and well-perfused. No C/C/E.

Neuro:
Mental status: Awake and alert, cooperative with exam, normal
affect.
Orientation: Oriented to person, place, and date.
Language: Speech fluent with good comprehension and repetition.

Cranial Nerves:
I: Not tested
II: Pupils equally round and reactive to light, to 4/2mm
bilaterally. Visual fields are full to confrontation.
III, IV, VI: Extraocular movements intact bilaterally without
nystagmus.
V, VII: Facial strength and sensation intact and symmetric.
VIII: Hearing intact to finger rub bilaterally.
IX, X: Palatal elevation symmetrical.
Charles: Sternocleidomastoid and trapezius normal bilaterally.
XII: Tongue midline without fasciculations.

Motor: Normal bulk and tone bilaterally. No abnormal movements,
tremors. Strength full power 04-12 throughout. No pronator drift

Sensation: Intact to light touch b/l.

Reflexes: B T Br Pa Ac
Right 2 2 2 2 2
Left 2 2 2 2 2

Toes downgoing bilaterally

Coordination: slow finger-nose-finger, slow rapid alternating
movements.


Pertinent Results:
LABS:
WBC: 11.4  Hgb  11.0  Hct: 31.7  Plt 369
Na  K  Cl  HCO3  BUN  CR  Glu
PT 12.4  PTT 26.9 INR 1.1

CT/MRI:
CT HEAD w/o CONTRAST
IMPRESSION:
1.  A small amount of subarachnoid hemorrhage within the left
parietal region.
2.  Large amount of soft tissue swelling over the right temporal
region.  No underlying fracture identified.
3.  Mild exophthalmus bilaterally with straightening of the
optic
nerves consistent with proliferation of the retro-orbital fat.
This can be seen in underlying thyroid disease or steroids use -
clinical correlation is recommended.


Brief Hospital Course:
Pt was admitted through the ED s/p fall with TBI.  Pt was
started on dilantin prophylactically for seuizure prevention.
Pt was advanced in diet and activity. She has remained stable,
her Ct scans have been stable.  The plan is to discharge to home
and continue on dilantin until seen in f/u clinic in 4 weeks.
Pt is tolerating an oral diet and voiding freely - she is
ambulatory without difficulty - she agrees with the plan to go
home.

Medications on Admission:
Medications prior to admission: Vasotec, Lipitor, Glucophage,
Celexa, Anafranil, Klonopin, Risperdal.


Discharge Medications:
1. Enalapril Maleate 5 mg Tablet Sig: Four (4) Tablet PO BID (2
times a day).
2. Artificial Tear with Lanolin 0.1-0.1 % Ointment Sig: One (1)
Appl Ophthalmic  PRN (as needed).
Disp:*1 1* Refills:*2*
3. Atorvastatin 20 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY
(Daily).
4. Citalopram 20 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY (Daily).

5. Clonazepam 0.5 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO BID (2 times
a day).
6. Clomipramine 25 mg Capsule Sig: Two (2) Capsule PO DAILY
(Daily).
7. Risperidone 1 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO BID (2 times a
day).
8. Docusate Sodium 100 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO TID (3
times a day).
Disp:*90 Capsule(s)* Refills:*2*
9. Glipizide 10 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY (Daily).

10. Metformin 500 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO BID (2 times
a day).
11. Oxycodone-Acetaminophen 5-325 mg Tablet Sig: 1-2 Tablets PO
Q4-6H (every 4 to 6 hours) as needed.
Disp:*60 Tablet(s)* Refills:*0*
12. Phenytoin Sodium Extended 100 mg Capsule Sig: Two (2)
Capsule PO BID (2 times a day).
Disp:*120 Capsule(s)* Refills:*2*


Discharge Disposition:
Home

Discharge Diagnosis:
Traumatic head injury


Discharge Condition:
neurologically stable


Discharge Instructions:
Call or go to ER for severe headache or mental status changes.
Call if you have any persistent dizziness nause or vomiting or
headache that is worsening.  you can call the office at
867-164-2669 if you have any questions or concerns.

Followup Instructions:
Follow up with Dr. Morgan and  head CT in 4 weeks - call
104-797-3628 for appt.



Completed by:2018-05-08
